title: cornus alba f. spaethii
intro: Cornus alba f. spaethii, also known as Spaeth's dogwood or Redosier dogwood, is a deciduous shrub in the flowering plant family Cornaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been reported to possess medicinal properties. The health benefits of Cornus alba f. spaethii include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made from the bark, leaves, or roots, and are often used to treat various health conditions, such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues.
